methodsAttempted.query: site:universite-paris-saclay.fr Université Paris-Saclay IA générative enseignement étudiants charte
methodsAttempted.notes: Found official pages about IAG working-group activity, AI teaching events, and course/training material; retained the working-group article for narrow non-binding process claims.
Confirmed the IAG working-group article and rejected event/news-only pages as insufficient direct claim support for binding policy.
Found official 2025-2026 first-cycle exam-regulation PDF with explicit ChatGPT/AI source-disclosure and sanction language.
Found official 2025-2026 master exam-regulation PDF with the same explicit ChatGPT/AI source-disclosure and sanction language.
Confirmed the official IAG working-group page; third-party and search-index pages were not used as evidence.

artifacts.summary: Firecrawl discovery and fetches found official French-language Université Paris-Saclay evidence in two 2025-2026 exam-regulation PDFs and one IAG working-group article. Event announcements, training catalogues, and course pages were rejected or excluded from approved claim support when they did not provide binding or directly scoped policy evidence.

artifacts.claimText: Université Paris-Saclay's 2025-2026 first-cycle exam rules say that, for Licence professionnelle, Licence, and Licence double-diplôme students covered by the rules, use of ChatGPT or another AI tool must be explicitly mentioned when it is not prohibited, and failure to mention AI as a source will be sanctioned.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Règlement des études, MCC et charte des examens des formations de 1er cycle de l'Université Paris-Saclay 2025-2026
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Les règles présentées dans ce document s'appliquent à tous les étudiants inscrits en Licence professionnelle, Licence et Licence double-diplôme de l'Université Paris-Saclay. L'utilisation de ChatGPT ou tout autre outil ayant recours à l'IA (Intelligence Artificielle) devra être mentionnée de façon explicite (quand elle n'est pas interdite), comme n'importe quel emprunt ou citation d'une source externe. Le non-respect de la mention de l'IA comme source se verra sanctionné.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The rules apply to Licence professionnelle, Licence, and Licence double-diplôme students; ChatGPT or other AI use must be explicitly mentioned when not prohibited, and failure to mention AI as a source will be sanctioned.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Partie II scope; Partie III - Charte des examens, 2. Plagiat et fraude

artifacts.title: Université Paris-Saclay staged AI policy crawl
artifacts.summary: Staged crawl for Université Paris-Saclay (QS 2026 rank =70; France) found official French-language evidence in 2025-2026 first-cycle and master exam-regulation PDFs for ChatGPT/AI source-disclosure and sanction language, plus an official IAG working-group article for non-binding process and guidance-development claims. All claims are staged candidates only and require human review before canonical publication.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
This staged run does not publish canonical claims and does not mark any item human_reviewed.
No final university-wide generative-AI charter was found in this crawl; the official event page mentioning a future charter was rejected as event-only support.
The IAG working-group claims are non-binding process/guidance-development claims; English display snippets are helper translations only, while French evidence snippets remain canonical.
No Playwright escalation was used because Firecrawl successfully fetched the official pages and PDFs.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
